events.title: Ebola outbreak follows hunger and displacement crisis in DR Congo
events.description: A day after the World Health Organization (WHO) declared the new Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo an international emergency, top global disease transmission experts stressed that the chances of another global pandemic similar to the 2019 coronavirus emergency are increasing all the time.

events.title: WHO considers use of experimental vaccines as Ebola cases and deaths rise in DRC
events.description: WHO chief said he was ‘deeply concerned’ after at least 500 suspected Ebola cases and 130 deaths reported in outbreak of Bundibugyo strain ‘It’s heartbreaking’: panic in eastern DRC over return of Ebola Global health leaders are considering whether vaccines or medicines still in development could be used to fight Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, as the World Health Organization’s chief said he was deeply concerned by the outbreak’s speed and scale. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said there had been at least 500 suspected cases of Ebola and 130 suspected deaths in DRC since the new outbreak began – up from about 200 cases and 65 deaths when it was announced on Friday . Continue reading...

events.title: Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a ‘Public Health Emergency of International Concern’
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, citing rising cases, cross-border spread and significant uncertainties about the scale of the epidemic.

events.title: 1.1 billion people live in slums. Can they be housed in dignity?
events.description: Ministers, mayors, international organisations, urban planners and experts are in Baku, Azerbaijan, to tackle one of the world’s fastest-growing challenges: the global housing crisis, which, according to the United Nations, affects nearly 2.8 billion people worldwide.

events.title: Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows
events.description: Rising volume of components imported from China prompts warning of cannibalisation of European industries Europe is facing a fresh China shock that threatens to cannibalise local factories, leading to job losses and de facto colonisation of industry by Beijing, trade analysts and representatives have said. They fear the plunging exchange rate and support for Chinese “zombie firms” has echoes of the crisis in the US 25 years ago when the term “China shock” was coined. It referred to the impact of China bursting on to the global trade stage after becoming a member of the World Trade Organization, with soaring imports displacing local industries and causing the loss of up to 2.5m jobs. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows

events.title: Global energy and trade disruption pushing millions towards poverty
events.description: Disruptions to global energy supplies and trade corridors are driving up the cost of food, transport and essential goods worldwide, slowing economic growth and increasing pressure on vulnerable households and debt-strapped developing countries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global energy and trade disruption pushing millions towards poverty

events.title: 'Russia and China share same goal in constructing alternative world order', analyst says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Rex Li, Research Affiliate of Lau China Institute and Professor of International Relations at the School of Global Affairs King's College London, says that Vladimir Putin has been "warmly welcomed by Chinese leaders" compared to Donald Trump, explaining that "Russia and China share the same goal of building a multi-polar world and constructing an alternative world order".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Russia and China share same goal in constructing alternative world order', analyst says

events.title: Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean
events.description: Record-breaking temperatures, deadly floods, worsening drought and intensifying hurricanes are placing millions of people across Latin America and the Caribbean at growing risk of hunger, displacement and water shortages, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean

events.title: WHO concerned by 'scale and speed' of Ebola spread in DRC
events.description: Congo's Health Ministry has logged 131 suspected deaths and 513 potential cases in the latest outbreak in the country's east. The World Health Organization is holding an emergency meeting on Tuesday in Geneva.

events.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a public health emergency after 80 suspected deaths
events.description: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda an international public health emergency after dozens of suspected deaths were reported.

events.title: At least one killed, 21 wounded in car bomb explosion in Syria’s Damascus
events.description: Blast occurs near Defence Ministry building in Bab Sharqi district of the capital.
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least one killed, 21 wounded in car bomb explosion in Syria’s Damascus

events.title: NSW police charge two men after allegedly seizing more than $1m of illicit tobacco – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Global Sumud Flotilla participants urge Australian government to intervene and ‘keep us safe’ The Australians that the Global Sumud Flotilla allege have been kidnapped by the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) are academics, doctors, students, activists and film-makers. This is now the second time Israel has illegally abducted Australian citizens in international waters, and our government has said nothing … For as long as Australia continues to support Israel’s genocide, Australians will keep sailing. You never know the minute when the Israelis decide that ‘yeah, let’s fuck over some ordinary people,’ 500 of them, who have got a whole load of baby food in boats trying to break their illegal siege in international waters. Here we are in international waters, and our governments are completely failing us. Do everything that you can to keep us safe. When people are unwell with colds or flu, they can’t donate, even if they want to. But the need for blood doesn’t slow down. Patients undergoing cancer treatment, people needing surgery, and those injured in accidents rely on blood every single day. Continue reading...

events.title: At least 22 killed in Israeli attacks on Lebanon in 24 hours: Ministry
events.description: Lebanon death toll since March 2 has reached 3,042, according to the country's Health Ministry.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 At least 22 killed in Israeli attacks on Lebanon in 24 hours: Ministry

events.title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku as housing crisis and climate shocks intensify
events.description: Soaring housing costs, climate shocks and conflicts are leaving millions without adequate shelter – but what can be done? As the 13th UN World Urban Forum opens on Sunday in Baku, Azerbaijan, participants will grapple with solutions to a deepening global housing crisis.

events.title: WHO head 'deeply concerned' over 'scale and speed' of Ebola spread, says emergency committee will meet
events.description: WHO chief convenes emergency committee over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, citing over 500 suspected cases and 131 fatalities from the virus.

events.title: World News in Brief: More attacks in Ukraine, violence against children in Haiti, refugee IDs in Africa
events.description: Civilians, including humanitarians, continue to face great danger across war-torn Ukraine amid ongoing hostilities, according to the UN humanitarian relief coordination office there, OCHA.

events.title: ‘Foolish’ CSIRO job cuts will mean Australia unable to provide climate projections to global reports, scientists warn
events.description: Exclusive: Science agency is planning to sack a third of the team working on the national climate model, sources say Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Job cuts at the national science agency mean Australia will no longer be able to submit climate projections to form part of global reports and will have significantly reduced ability to forecast future damage to the country, leading researchers have warned. Multiple sources told Guardian Australia that CSIRO planned to sack a third of the team working on the national climate model that provides projections relied on by governments, councils, industry and farmers as they plan for the future. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Foolish’ CSIRO job cuts will mean Australia unable to provide climate projections to global reports, scientists warn

events.title: Congo health minister announces 3 Ebola treatment centers amid ongoing outbreak
events.description: Congo will open three treatment centers for the Ebola virus in the eastern Ituri province, and the World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the country, following an outbreak that has killed nearly 120 people

events.title: Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages
events.description: Nearly 20 million people across Sudan are facing acute hunger and more than 800,000 children risk severe malnutrition this year, UN agencies warned on Friday, as civil war, mass displacement and collapsing food and health systems deepen one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises and push parts of the country closer to famine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages

Continue reading...

events.title: US, Iran trade threats as Tehran stages mass weddings for couples ready for 'sacrifice'
events.description: President Donald Trump warned Tuesday that the United States may strike Iran again, but Tehran's army threatened to open "new fronts" if he went ahead. In the capital, Iranian authorities held mass public weddings for couples who signed up to a state-sponsored scheme declaring their readiness to sacrifice their lives in the war against the US and Israel. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US, Iran trade threats as Tehran stages mass weddings for couples ready for 'sacrifice'

events.title: As WHO sounds alarm over Ebola in DRC, what can be learned from previous outbreaks?
events.description: Conflict, mistrust and delayed detection could complicate response to emergency caused by Bundibugyo variant To be around the centre of an Ebola outbreak is to become used to the smell of chlorine. At hospitals and government buildings, surfaces are sprayed with it and hands washed in a 0.05% solution that can kill the virus in 60 seconds. Infrared handheld thermometers take temperatures at airports and border crossings. Any indication of a fever prevents passage. Contact-tracing teams crisscross the countryside. Continue reading...

events.title: Ghana hopes to attract investors after €3 billion IMF bailout ends
events.description: Ghana's three billion dollar IMF bailout has just wrapped up, and it's now hoping that it has a better financial footing to tempt investors over. The country needed the support to ride its worst economic downturn in decades following the pandemic, Russia's invasion of Ukraine and inflation. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo is on the ground and spoke to Ghanaians about the last three years.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ghana hopes to attract investors after €3 billion IMF bailout ends

events.title: Housing crisis takes centre stage at World Urban Forum in Baku
events.description: Now is the time to place housing at the heart of sustainable development. That was the message delivered on Monday by UN Secretary General António Guterres to participants at a global forum on urban sustainability under way this week in Baku.

events.title: Woman shot dead by police in Jamaica at protest over previous police shooting

Footage circulating on social media shows a police officer firing at a vehicle during a protest on Sunday in Granville, in Jamaica’s north-western parish of St James. The bullet hit Latoya Bulgin, 45, who was behind the wheel of the vehicle. Continue reading...

events.title: Brush fire near Los Angeles forces thousands to evacuate

events.title: Vladimir Putin arrives in Beijing for state visit hot on heels of Trump
events.description: Russian leader says relations between two countries have reached ‘unprecedented level’ on eve of 25th visit to China Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing for a state visit, four days after Donald Trump left China. The Russian leader’s visit to China – his 25th, according to Chinese state media – reflects Beijing’s growing confidence on the world stage as a centre of global diplomatic activity. Continue reading...

events.title: Starbucks Korea CEO dismissed over ad evoking massacre of pro-democracy protesters
events.description: ‘Tank Day’ event causes outrage with ‘malicious mockery’ of deadly crackdown during dictatorship era The chief executive of Starbucks in South Korea has been dismissed after the company ran a promotional event using slogans that evoked a massacre of pro-democracy protesters during the country’s dictatorship era, sparking outrage and boycott calls. The coffee chain launched a “Tank Day” campaign on 18 May for its “Tank” tumbler series. The date coincides with one of the most politically sensitive days in South Korea’s calendar, when citizens commemorate the 1980 democratisation movement in Gwangju, 167 miles (270km) south-west of Seoul. Continue reading...

events.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency

According to the Kremlin, Putin and Xi Jinping are set to discuss how to "further strengthen" Russia and China's strategic partnership and "exchange views on key international and regional issues".

events.title: Rubio criticizes WHO’s Ebola response as US continues sweeping public health cuts
events.description: US secretary of state says WHO was ‘a little late’ in identifying deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda US secretary of state Marco Rubio said on Tuesday that the World Health Organization (WHO) was “a little late” in identifying the deadly Ebola outbreak in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. On Tuesday, Rubio told reporters: “The lead is obviously going to be CDC (the Centers for Disease Control) and the World Health Organization, which was a little late to identify this thing unfortunately.” Continue reading...

events.title: 'Liquid on lips': Noughty makes premium alcohol-free wine really nice & 'appealing to wine drinkers'
events.description: Amanda Thomson, a former BBC correspondent turned award-winning entrepreneur with a Champagne taste for innovation, founded Noughty, a premium alcohol-free wine brand, in 2019. She set out to redefine one of France’s most cherished institutions with a deceptively radical premise: offering wine lovers across the globe a taste of the good life while always remaining in good spirits. In doing so, she brings a distinctly modern twist to one of the world’s most treasured traditions. Thomson sees herself as an architect of a seismic shift within the wine industry.

The state’s former security minister Gerardo Mérida Sánchez crossed the border into Arizona last week and was taken into custody by US marshals, Mexico’s security ministry said. events.title: Eldest son of Mango fashion empire founder held in murder inquiry into father's death

Jonathan was alone with his 71-year-old billionaire father when the clothing empire founder plunged to his death in the Montserrat mountains near Barcelona on December 14, 2024. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland has more.

events.title: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit
events.description: President’s remarks about Taiwan arms deals being a ‘negotiating chip’ with Beijing have been seized on by Chinese state media It has been an unsettling few days for Taiwan’s government. When Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday, many feared the unpredictable US leader could upend Washington’s longstanding support for Taipei. But beyond a starkly worded statement from Xi stressing China’s claims over Taiwan, which it claims as part of its territory despite never having ruled it, initial signs appeared good for Taipei. events.title: France to return bodies of Indigenous people exhibited in colonial ‘human zoos’
events.description: French senators have unanimously passed a bill allowing for the remains of people who were exhibited in colonial-era “human zoos” in Paris to be returned to their ancestral lands in French Guiana, on the northeast coast of South America. The draft law meets a long-standing demand of Indigenous communities in France’s overseas territories, acknowledging a dark chapter from the country’s past.

events.title: Putin on the back foot? Russian president in China as Ukraine campaign stalls

Days after Donald Trump left Beijing, Vladimir Putin’s turn in the Chinese capital, where the Russian president can boast that unlike his U-S counterpart, he’ll be signing actual business deals. But the biggest one may have to wait. Xi Jinping in no hurry to agree to the Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline which would double Russia’s exports to China.

events.title: From favelas to informal settlements, new UN report points to housing solutions that work

Refugees and host communities are reclaiming neglected spaces, turning them greener and safer. In Brazil, favelas are being upgraded rather than torn down, while in Germany, rent controls are helping to steady the market.

events.title: Spanish ex-PM Zapatero investigated over airline bailout
events.description: A Spanish court says it is probing ex-Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling. The case puts more pressure on the current premier, Pedro Sanchez, who is dogged by corruption probes.

events.title: South Korea, Japan agree to boost energy cooperation
events.description: The South Korean and Japanese leaders, whose nations have not always had good relations, have vowed deeper ties at talks. They reaffirmed stronger security coordination and energy cooperation in challenging times.

events.title: Freiburg's Europa League final proof another way can work
events.description: Bundesliga side Freiburg are in their first ever European final. Their European run is a reminder that success in football can be achieved without big spending and without sacrifcing identity.

events.title: China's moment? Putin heads to Beijing after Trump courts Xi
events.description: Just days after hosting Donald Trump, China's Xi Jinping is set to welcome Russian leader Vladimir Putin. The meetings underscore Beijing's growing role in a fractured geopolitical landscape.

events.title: Pedro Almodóvar chases elusive Palme d’Or as Andy García returns to Cannes with noir passion project
events.description: Spanish author Pedro Almodóvar returns to Competition at the Cannes Film Festival with "Bitter Christmas", a self-reflective drama about filmmaking and mental collapse. Meanwhile, Russian director Andrey Zvyagintsev premieres the gripping "Minotaur" dealing with tensions in Russia, while Andy García unveils "Diamond" – his long-awaited noir passion project starring Vicky Krieps and Brendan Fraser.

events.title: Japan’s maid cafés: From niche subculture to tourist draw
events.description: Emerging in the early 2000s, Japan’s maid cafés, where waitresses in French maid’s costumes serve and chat with customers, were once seen as niche subculture hangouts for a mainly male clientele. But they have now evolved into a mainstream attraction and are increasingly drawing foreign visitors to Japan.

events.title: Kentucky primary tests Trump's hold on Republican base
events.description: President Donald Trump is targeting one of his strongest critics within the Republican Party: Representative Thomas Massie, currently running in a primary race in Kentucky. The contest has become the most expensive US House of representatives primary in history, and will test Trump's hold over the Republican party, after he successfully pushed out another key critic in Louisiana.

events.title: Bodies of divers who died exploring Maldives cave recovered
events.description: The bodies of two Italian divers who died while exploring a cave in the Maldives have been recovered.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bodies of divers who died exploring Maldives cave recovered

events.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI
events.description: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against OpenAI and its chief executive Sam Altman.

events.title: Irish president's sister detained by Israel, aid flotilla organisers say
events.description: The sister of Irish President Catherine Connolly is said to be among at least six Irish citizens on an aid flotilla to Gaza to have been detained by Israel off the coast of Cyprus.

events.title: What to know about the political chaos engulfing the UK's Labour Party and efforts to unseat Starmer
events.description: Beleaguered British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is trying to return to the business of running government

events.title: Greenland's PM insists on self-determination in meeting with Trump's envoy
events.description: PM Nielsen says a meeting with Trump's special envoy to the Arctic was positive.

events.title: Divers will try to recover 2 of the dead Italians from an Maldives underwater cave
events.description: Three Finnish divers will attempt to recover the bodies of two of the four Italians who died deep inside an underwater cave in an atoll in the Maldives
events.ticker_label: 💥 Divers will try to recover 2 of the dead Italians from an Maldives underwater cave

events.title: Lettuce introduce you to the live frog found in this grocery store salad bag
events.description: An Australian farmer has found a live frog in a bag of lettuce

events.title: A humanitarian aid ship from Mexico docks in Havana as US-Cuba tensions escalate
events.description: A ship carrying humanitarian aid from Mexico and Uruguay has arrived in Havana to help ease Cuba’s spiraling crises

events.title: Slovenia set for a right-wing government and a comeback for former Prime Minister Jansa
events.description: Right-wing politician Janez Jansa is set to become Slovenia's prime minister again after his candidacy was submitted to Parliament on Tuesday

events.title: Prince William's Duchy of Cornwall to sell 20% of property portfolio to fund housing, green projects
events.description: Prince William’s Duchy of Cornwall plans to sell about 20% of its property portfolio over the next decade

events.title: Turning silence into stories: Cambodian activist amplifies LGBTQI+ youth voices
events.description: Cambodian activist Panha Theng, a 2025 UN Young Leader for the Sustainable Development Goals, says visibility and safe spaces remain critical for LGBTQI+ youth across Southeast Asia amid continuing stigma and discrimination.

events.title: California doctor convicted of $45M Medicare fraud scheme that funded luxury vacations and a $12K crossbow
events.description: California doctor accused of submitting $45 million in false Medicare claims for Botox injections was convicted of wire fraud and obstruction charges.

events.title: Second batch of UFO files set to be released after lawmaker teased ‘Holy Crap’ moment
events.description: The Trump administration is expected to release a second wave of declassified UAP files very soon as part of its PURSUE transparency program.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Second batch of UFO files set to be released after lawmaker teased ‘Holy Crap’ moment

events.title: New York's 'wrong-headed' defendant-friendly laws helped toss Mangione evidence: retired cop
events.description: Judge ruled Pennsylvania police conducted an unconstitutional search of Luigi Mangione's backpack, tossing key evidence in the murder case against him.

events.title: Tyler Robinson's defense team fights to seal evidence and punish prosecutors in Charlie Kirk's assassination
events.description: Tyler Robinson, accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk at a Utah event, faces court hearing on defense motions to seal evidence and close testimony.

events.title: Alabama boy battling cancer lands experimental drug after posting secret viral video that drew Trump response
events.description: An Alabama teen battling an aggressive form of bone cancer has started experimental therapy after a secretly posted Facebook video drew attention and support from high-profile figures.

events.title: Cold case killer who chewed cops’ undercover DNA bait faces survivor, families in courtroom showdown
events.description: A Washington man was sentenced to 50 years to life after a piece of gum linked him to two cold case murders dating back to the 1980s in Everett.

events.title: Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died
events.description: Uyghur mother Mihrigul Tursun recounts years of alleged detention, torture and family separation in China's Xinjiang camps in emotional testimony.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died
